title: (Senior) Financial Risk Management Officer - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The **EIB** , the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Group Risk \& Compliance Directorate (GR\&C), Group Financial Risk Department (GFIN), ALM and Market Risk Division (ALM), Market Risk and Pricing Control Unit (MRC) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a ****(Senior) Financial Risk Management Officer**** **.** **This is a full\-time position at grade 5/6 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.** ***Panel interviews are anticipated for April 2026*** Purpose You will develop and maintain robust risk policies and frameworks in the domain of pricing and market risk aligned with the Bank’s Asset and Liability Management (ALM) framework as well as best banking practices and regulations in order to ensure the proactive identification, assessment and oversight of risk exposures. Operating Network You will report to the Head of Market Risk and Pricing Control Unit and work closely with the risk officers within the division, as well as the Departments involved in financial and control matters, especially with the Finance Directorate, the Directorate for Lending Operations and Financial Control. You will interact, where appropriate, with external counterparts and both Internal and External Auditors. Accountabilities * Develop and implement the 2nd LoD oversight framework for loan pricing (specific to financial risk pricing components) and market risk (Interest Rates, FX and Pension risks). * Prepare risk assessments related to the Bank’s Interest Rate Strategy. * Validate that the calculation of financial risk metrics of the EIB Group/EIF portfolios is consistent with other reports produced by Finance Directorate and/or Chief Financial Controller Directorate. * Drive innovation within the division by championing existing initiatives and fostering the development of new, high\-impact ideas. * Ensure the integration and testing of new product representations within financial risk systems. * Steer the continuous enhancement of ALM and Financial Risk policies to align with evolving market practices, regulatory expectations and the Bank’s strategic risk appetite. * Ensure compliance with approved procedures and policies. * Lead the risk assessment of mandate\-related activities by delivering Management Committee opinions, overseeing the underlying financial risks and designing operational buffers for covering those risks. **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor), preferably in Economics, Finance, Mathematics or another quantitative subject. Post\-graduate studies in these areas would be an advantage. * At least 5 years of relevant professional experience in Finance, ideally ALM, Risk Management, Capital Markets, or Treasury functions. * Proven expertise in quantitative and financial modelling, including loan pricing and market risk sensitivities * Demonstrated experience in implementing models and methodologies supporting the monitoring and management of market risks in banking books. * Good communication and interpersonal skills. * Programming knowledge (e.g Python) would be an advantage. * Experience with IT systems such as WSS (FK), Business Objects, Power BI and CompatibL is a plus. * Thorough understanding of financial markets and relevant regulations, including money markets, capital markets, and derivatives. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage). Competencies **About** *Find out more about EIB core behavioural skills* here To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* *level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. Deadline for applications: **31st March 2026**

title: AOS Senior
description: **AOS Senior** Forvis Mazars Grow. Belong. Impact. Forvis Mazars in Ireland is a leading professional services firm with ambitious growth plans and over 1000 staff based in Cork, Dublin, Galway and Limerick. We are an integral part of Forvis Mazars Group, an internationally integrated partnership with over 40,000 professionals operating in over 100 countries and territories around the world. Our strategic framework drives positive and meaningful impact for our people, the environment and local communities while delivering unmatched client experience to our clients. **Why join us** Our people are ambassadors and leaders and have a bold entrepreneurial spirit to shape the future of our industry and the communities within which we serve. We empower and develop our teams to become professionals of the highest calibre in technical and client excellence. Our people first approach offers our teams a caring work environment which promotes belonging and inclusivity of all cultures and perspectives. **The Position** We have an exciting opportunity for an experienced accountant with a strong financial background to join our leading international firm and specifically our award\-winning Financial Accounting and Outsourcing Services Division. Our Financial Accounting and Outsourcing team support our clients to better run their finance functions, understand their financial position and performance, and to ensure they are compliant with changing accounting and tax legislation. The successful candidate will work with a diverse range of clients across various industries, assisting with all aspects of their accounting lifecycle. This ambitious individual will thrive on working as part of a dynamic team and delivering first in class client services. **Key Responsibilities** The successful candidate will be responsible for the following: * Ensuring adherence to client and filing deadlines through planning, scheduling and overseeing individual client projects * Managing a diverse portfolio of clients. * Prepare management accounts, VAT returns (inc VAT, VIES and Intrastat Returns), statutory financial statements, complete reconciliations, and lead book keeping projects, secondment roles, forecasting and budgeting and other ad hoc department projects. * Oversight of billing and fees including budgeting and the timely preparation of invoices. * Drive high\-quality service delivery within expected timeframes and on budget. * Bring technical expertise and a strong knowledge and application of various accounting standards, primarily, FRS 102 * Ensure appropriate maintenance of financial information for a broad range of clients using various accounting software packages including Xero and Sage 50 * Creating and updating financial statements as required and in conjunction with the team using accounts production software * Remaining up to date with all accounting and regulatory standards to ensure compliance with all legislative practices. * Liaising with various external stakeholders providing support as required including external auditors as part of the year end audit process for individual clients * Building strong relationships with client representatives * Manage expectations of client service delivery * Foster an innovative and inclusive team\-oriented work environment and play an active role in mentoring junior team members within the organisation **Ideal Candidate** * Qualified ACA/ACCA * Experienced senior accounts assistant/ accountant in a professional services or industry environment (2\+ years) * An ambitious individual with a desire to deliver strong client services and grow and develop a team * Proficient in Microsoft Word and Excel with a willingness to learn new systems * Meticulous attention to detail, accuracy and the ability to manage multiple engagements * Excellent time management skills with an ability to work effectively to deadlines * Ability to work effectively as part of a team * A self\-motivated individual with an ability to work on their own initiative with an awareness for when to seek assistance and work collaboratively. * Excellent interpersonal skills, particularly communication skills, both written and verbal * Energy, enthusiasm and passion for a new challenge. * Ability to learn quickly with an interest in legislative developments including local GAAP, IFRS and US GAAP, VAT changes etc. Don’t wait. Act. Please Note: Forvis Mazars is an Equal Opportunities Employer. In applying for a role with us, you consent that Forvis Mazars will process your personal data for the purpose of handling your application. Forvis Mazars endeavours to recruit and fill vacancies directly. However, at times when we do need to engage with agencies, Forvis Mazars operates within a preferred supplier list (PSL) and only work and partner with recruitment suppliers where our agreed contractual terms are in place. If unsolicited CVs are received from agencies, we will not be liable for payment of introduction fees. Thank you for your co\-operation.

title: AVP, International Tax
description: With a company culture rooted in collaboration, expertise and innovation, we aim to promote progress and inspire our clients, employees, investors and communities to achieve their greatest potential. Our work is the catalyst that helps others achieve their goals. In short, We Enable Possibility℠. The **primary purpose** of this position is to support the International Tax team on a broad range of complex tax matters across EMEA including, M\&A, special projects, transfer pricing, tax controversy and, to a lesser extent, direct tax compliance and US GAAP tax reporting. **International Tax Strategy** * Support the development and implementation of international tax strategies while maintaining compliance with relevant laws and regulations * Conduct technical research and provide analysis to senior leaders on the tax implications of international business activities, reinsurance arrangements, and cross‑border capital flows * Assist in assessing the tax impact of mergers, acquisitions, expansions, and corporate restructurings; contribute to the execution of functional or legal entity restructuring projects * Monitor international tax legislative developments and communicate potential business impacts to relevant stakeholders **Compliance, Reporting \& Governance** * Manage direct tax compliance across relevant jurisdictions ensuring accurate and timely filings and audit readiness * Assist in maintaining and updating global transfer pricing documentation, intercompany agreements, and compliance requirements under OECD and local rules * Contribute to process improvements and tax technology/automation initiatives to enhance accuracy, efficiency, and governance in tax processes **Risk Management** * Assist in identifying and assessing international tax risks associated with cross‑border transactions, including reinsurance and other complex structures * Support the maintenance of strong processes and internal controls to ensure adherence to the company’s tax governance framework **Stakeholder Collaboration** * Work with Finance, Legal, Treasury and other internal teams to ensure tax considerations are incorporated into operational and transactional activities * Liaise with external advisors, regulators, and auditors to support the management of international tax matters and audits * Support senior leaders in preparation for interactions with tax authorities or participation in industry forums **Team Support \& Development** * Provide guidance and support to junior team members as needed, fostering collaboration and knowledge sharing within the international tax team * Assist in managing project workstreams on complex tax matters, ensuring accurate analysis, timely execution, and alignment with broader project objectives **Qualifications / Education** * Bachelor’s degree in accounting, economics, finance or law from an accredited college/university * Licensed CPA/CA/CTA or similar **Skills / Competencies** * Strong knowledge of international tax rules, treaty analysis, BEPS frameworks, and international transfer pricing standards * Strong analytical, communication, decision making and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the capability to influence senior executives and cross‑functional stakeholders * While primarily a direct tax role, the ability to identify indirect tax and employee tax issues is important given the coverage of this role **Experience** * Minimum five years international tax experience in industry and/or in a public accounting firm * Non\-life insurance industry experience is an advantage * Demonstrated experience in regional/global tax planning, international compliance, cross‑border structuring, and transfer pricing * Prior leadership experience managing teams and complex international engagements * Experience and knowledge of tax reporting within the framework of U.S. GAAP and/or IFRS financial reporting is desired but not essential Do you like solving complex business problems, working with talented colleagues and have an innovative mindset? Arch may be a great fit for you. If this job isn’t the right fit but you’re interested in working for Arch, create a job alert! Simply create an account and opt in to receive emails when we have job openings that meet your criteria. Join our talent community to share your preferences directly with Arch’s Talent Acquisition team. 14101 Arch Europe Insurance Services Ltd

title: Air Freight Operations Graduate
description: **DHL Global Forwarding Graduate Programme 2026** **As a Graduate** , you’ll use your talents to create innovative solutions and shape the future across our business. Join a global network of passionate experts – backed by the latest technology, to develop your career to management level. The DGF Graduate Scheme places you in a variety of roles across our international Freight Forwarding business. Throughout the scheme you will be empowered to learn on the job and deliver real results. We offer a supportive culture in a highly engaged workforce and provide investment in your training to ensure that you get the most out of your graduate scheme. **What the scheme looks like:** 24‑month structured scheme A comprehensive operational development programme including exposure to: * Airfreight export documentation and compliance * Customer communication and relationship management * Booking management, transport scheduling and shipment coordination * End‑to‑end job file ownership support * Global network communication with DHL overseas offices * Air Cargo Security processes and Known Shipper checks * Performance and KPI management * Billing accuracy, invoicing support and cost checking * Exception management and incident reporting * Cross‑functional collaboration across Warehouse, Customer Service, Finance and Global Network teams You will develop gradually, starting with supported tasks and progressing toward independently handling export shipments. **Skills, Experience \& Educational Qualifications required:** * 2:1 or above degree (predicted or obtained) in Logistics, Supply Chain, Business, Transport Management, Aviation Management, or related discipline * Interest in airfreight operations, logistics and international trade * Motivation to develop a career within Freight Forwarding * Passion for excellent customer service and operational accuracy * Enthusiastic, open‑minded, with a strong ‘Can‑Do’ attitude * Confident communicator able to work with customers, carriers and global teams * Eligibility to work in Ireland * Fluent in English **Additional Personal Qualities:** * Strong attention to detail and accuracy in documentation and data handling * Organised, reliable and capable of managing time‑sensitive tasks * Calm under pressure when handling urgent shipments * Proactive problem‑solver who is eager to learn * Team‑oriented, respectful and collaborative * Willingness to learn operational systems, regulatory processes and SOPs **What do we offer?** The scheme will initially be a 24‑month Fixed Term Contract (FTC), with the aim that our Graduates move into a permanent position within the business – subject to a suitable vacancy being available and in line with the selection process for the role. **What will you get in return?** * Access to a variety of our excellent benefits, including 21 days’ holiday, bank holidays, service days, pension scheme, gym membership, discounted private healthcare, education assistance programme and family leave supports. * Early responsibility and fast progression. * Experience working within a fast‑paced environment. * Regular performance feedback and personal development planning. * Management Team Mentor and DGF Buddy. * Competitive salary. * You will be working for an accredited Top Employer and a certified Great Place to Work. **Location:** Dublin Based – Cedar Drive, Dublin Airport Logistics Park, St. Margaret’s, Co. Dublin **Hours:** 37\.5 hours per week **Closing:** TBC

title: Analyst, Corporate Banking, Dublin
description: Requisition ID: 255652 Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high\-performing culture. ***Purpose*** Contributes to the overall success of the Corporate Banking Department in Ireland ensuring specific individual goals, plans, initiatives are executed / delivered in support of the team’s business strategies and objectives. Ensures all activities conducted are in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies and procedures. Responsible for executing on a broad range of transactions. Responsible for preparing client presentations and financial modeling. Work closely with the Associates/Associate Directors to support the Directors/MDs in managing client relationships in the Power, Utilities and Infrastructure (“PUI”) industry sector. Contribute to the overall success of the team ensuring specific individual goals, plans, and initiatives are executed / delivered in support of the team’s business strategies and objectives. Ensure all activities conducted are in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies and procedures. **Accountabilities** * Champion a client centric focused culture to deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems, and knowledge to deliver solutions that Scotiabank can provide across its footprint and generate revenue. **Relationship Management And Cross Collaboration Includes** * Coordinating with the various product groups and industry team within GBM, with focus on European clients * Developing and maintaining effective industry relationships with legal counsel, external parties, clients **Day\-to\-day Responsibilities Include The Following** * Evaluating and analyzing the financial needs of corporate clients * Creating marketing materials and presentations * Supporting transaction structuring and execution efforts, with increasing levels of responsibility and accountability over time * Identifying and developing business opportunities with clients * Performing comprehensive financial statement analysis and creating and maintaining models * Collecting, consolidating, and interpreting company and industry data * Financial and written analysis of companies and industries * Applying product and capital markets knowledge to help clients achieve their financial objectives * Understand how the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day\-to\-day activities and decisions. * Actively pursues effective and efficient operations of his/her respective areas, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day\-to\-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational risk, regulatory compliance risk, AML/ATF risk and conduct risk, including but not limited to responsibilities under the Operational Risk Management Framework, Regulatory Compliance Risk Management Framework, AML/ATF Global Handbook and the Code of Conduct. * Contributes to a high performance environment and fosters an inclusive work environment; supporting the vison/values/business strategy for the team. ***Competencies*** * Strictly adheres to rules, policies, and procedures * Treats all stakeholders with courtesy, dignity and respect ***Dimensions*** * The Analyst is expected to work as part of the Corporate Banking team receiving guidance and direction from Associates, Associate Directors and Directors. * Location: Dublin, Ireland * Loan facilities across PUI sector. **Education / Experience** * A recent graduate with a passion for Banking and Finance * A demonstrated interest and knowledge in the financial markets * A proven record of outstanding achievement in academic and extracurricular activities * An independent thinker and decision maker who is able to contribute effectively to the team's success * Superior interpersonal and analytical skills * Ability to meet the challenges presented by a stressful, constantly changing work environment * 2nd language in intermediate/ business level desirable e.g. Spanish, French Location(s): Ireland : Leinster : Dublin Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets. At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Assistant Manager - Aviation Advisory
description: **Job Description** The suitable candidate to join the dynamic and quick growing Aviation Advisory team will be a qualified accountant with previous accounting/audit experience, highly motivated, excellent communication skills and hold a positive attitude towards working in a team environment. It will be an attractive opportunity for an individual who can rise to a challenge, be adaptable and enjoy working in a dynamic environment. The individual will be based in our Dublin office and will be expected to be flexible to visit client offices to meet their demands and continue to deliver the best\-in\-class service to our clients. This is an opportunity for someone to work with the number one provider of Aviation Lease Services to the aircraft leasing industry in Dublin and be immersed into the industry with high calibre colleagues. This role provides individuals with the opportunity to become a key member of the team and play a part in the continued and successful growth of the business unit. It offers highly attractive career development and progression opportunities. **Role Responsibilities** * Deliver on financial report outputs (financial models, financial statements, analysis etc.); * Supervise and train more junior members of the team to ensure the firm’s standards are continuously achieved; * Analyse \& establish the root cause of data issues; * Foster strong client relationships through regular communication, understanding their business challenges, and identifying opportunities for value\-added services; and * Develop knowledge of core processes within aircraft leasing (maintenance reserves \& aircraft lease management); * Oversee the work of trainees and seniors on client finance engagements; and * Support \& manage projects \& initiatives. * ACA/ACCA qualified; * 4\-5 years experience; * Audit experience from a top\-ten firm would be advantageous; * Ability to interpret financial statements; * Experience of accounting standards \- IFRS and Irish GAAP; * Knowledge on US GAAP would be beneficial; * Internal audit experience would be advantageous; * Excellent written and verbal communication; * Advanced excel and analytical skills; * Ability to understand problems quickly with a growth mindset and identify solutions; * Experience of working as part of a team supporting others; and * Skilled in motivating and managing staff to successfully meet tight deadlines.

title: Associate - Asset Management & Investment Funds
description: **The Role** We are currently seeking an Associate to join the Investment Funds practice. Walkers is currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic, team\-orientated and motivated lawyer to join our highly regarded Asset Management and Investment Funds team in Dublin. Our practice is a recognised leader in its field and currently acts for many of the leading asset management firms globally. Our team is frequently involved in cutting edge cross\-border fund\-related transactions, often involving our colleagues across our 10 global offices. The successful candidate will join a dynamic, flexible, experienced and growing team of leading lawyers. You will be given the opportunity to work on a broad range of matters representing our global and Irish\-based clients. **Duties \& Responsibilities** You will have a diverse range of responsibilities including the following: * assisting with the provision of: (i) advice on the structuring and establishment of Irish domiciled investment funds; (ii) on\-going advice to (a) Irish funds authorised as UCITS, RIAIFs, ELTIFs and QIAIFs; (b) management companies authorised as UCITS ManCos, AIFMs, Super ManCos and Mega ManCos (those with MiFID top\-ups); and (c) asset managers, fund promoters, fund service providers including investment managers and advisers; * drafting and reviewing a wide range of documentation relevant to investment funds and their managers and negotiating agreements with service providers; * managing assigned matters and liaising with other team members and colleagues within the Irish office and globally as needed; * creating and maintaining relationships with client and industry contacts; * assisting with legal and regulatory development project management; and * assisting with the preparation of client advisories and updates and building your industry profile through their publication. This is an excellent opportunity to work in an internationally focused, professional organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development, mentoring and career development within a collegiate team\-based environment. **Skills \& Experience** We are open to hearing from those with prior experience in asset management and investment funds, currently practising in Ireland PQE 3\-5 years (including those interested in a move from in\-house into a law firm) or those interested in returning or relocating to Ireland. Additionally, we are looking for candidates who can demonstrate the following: * Collegiate and thoughtful team player; * Strong academic credentials; * Excellent analytical, technical and drafting skills; * A high level of commercial and business awareness; * A calm and measured approach to problem solving; * Ability to work independently and as part of a team; * Strong ability to grasp and project plan for new regulatory developments; * Strong communication skills; * Excellent attention to detail; * A proven track record in managing competing priorities and deadlines. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Competitive Salary * Supportive environment and open culture * Prominent international clients and challenging work * Working directly with partners * 'Dress for your day' dress code * Discretionary annual bonus scheme; * Generous annual leave * Agile Working Policy * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme and firm contribution after 6 months * Life Assurance * Paid Maternity \& Paternity leave * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees paid * Learning \& Development and Mentorship programmes * Educational bursaries and assistance * Client secondment opportunities * Social Committee, DEI Committee, CSR Committee and various sports, social and charity initiatives * Subsidised gym access / fitness classes * Employee Assistance Programme * VDU eye testing and medical screening * Wellness programme

title: Associate Director - Transport Planning
description: **Work with Us. Change the World.** At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations. There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us. **Job Description** **Start here. Grow here.** Are you ready to challenge the boundaries of what's possible? AECOM continues to expand their successful Transport Planning team in Dublin. As part of a wider growth initiative linked to our strategic plan, we are currently seeking to appoint an individual to join our Transport Planning Team as Associate Director. **About You** You are a highly motivated, organised, and proactive leader with a proven track record in delivering transport solutions. With extensive experience in leading diverse teams in a fast\-paced environment, you have excelled in planning and delivering projects mainly for public sector clients. As a leader, you are responsible for fostering a culture of success, engagement, and development within the team while ensuring the successful management of projects from inception to completion. *Here’s what you’ll do:* **Technical Excellence:** you will support technical excellence and business with specific focus on client engagement, project delivery and technical resource development. **Oversee:** This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to our leadership team's focus on people, clients, technical excellence, operational efficiencies and sustainable growth. **Deliver:** Drive the technical delivery of transport planning projects drawing expertise/resources from across AECOM UK \& I. Take responsibility for project delivery including technical focus, quality, programme, budget and resource management. **Collaboration:** Support to the wider Planning Ireland business as Project Manager for some of our key projects with responsibility for programme, budget and resource management. Collaborate with project managers and national Technical Experts to identify/allocate resources effectively and optimise delivery performance. **Come grow with us.** We develop projects from concept through to operation providing engineering and construction services for multiyear, multimillion\-dollar projects for major rail and transit agencies. Our specialists provide analytical and strategic expertise for every stage of a project And let's not forget about the perks at AECOM. You'll enjoy a range of core and personalised benefits designed to support your future and well\-being, customized to fit your lifestyle. Take advantage of career development opportunities, our flexible hybrid working model to ensure a work\-life balance that suits your lifestyle, technical practice networks, AECOM University, and volunteering days. **Qualifications** **Ready to push the limits of what’s possible?** *Here’s what we’re looking for:* * Membership of a recognized, relevant professional body. * Min. Bachelor's degree in transport/urban planning or engineering, civil engineering, project management or a related field. * Experienced Associate Director, preferably within a multi\-disciplinary consultancy * Strong understanding of the transportation sector, regulatory and policy frameworks, and socio\-political drivers. * Demonstrated success in project management and technical excellence in transport planning. * Proven track record in developing and growing client relationships. * Strong experience in project appraisal and evaluation would be advantageous. We celebrate diversity, including neurodiversity, and believe it enriches our team. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and abilities. If you are an applicant with a disability that requires reasonable accommodation to complete the application process, please contact us at ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com At AECOM, we value everyone's unique contributions and perspectives. If you meet some of the requirements above or have transferable skills you believe would benefit us, we would be delighted to hear from you! **Additional Information** Interested in the role or curious about life at AECOM? Follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to explore our AECOM voices, employee stories, latest projects, and much more! **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Associate Director Property Management – Blanchardstown Centre
description: ***Associate Director – Blanchardstown Centre, Dublin.*** The Role / Location With an overall estate size of c. 85 acres, Blanchardstown Town Centre represents Ireland’s largest concentration of retail space, with 159,000 sq m of retail accommodation across the core shopping centre and two independent retail parks. The centre is anchored by Marks \& Spencer, Dunnes Stores, Boots and Zara. In total there are over 180 stores and restaurants, with 7,000 car parking spaces available on site. We are looking for an exceptional person to join our team at Associate Director level. The successful candidate will work with a team of two other Directors responsible for the management of Blanchardstown Centre, with the potential to support Directors on other large instructions. This role places particular emphasis on client and occupier relationship management, site team support and development and continuous improvement. Based at our head office and at Blanchardstown Centre, the successful candidate will be expected to work closely with the Centre Management team at Blanchardstown Centre, and will be required to identify and develop new business opportunities that will help continue the strong growth of our successful and expanding property \& facilities management business. **Key Responsibilities** For Blanchardstown Centre, the successful candidate will serve as the principal face of the surveying aspects of the running of the property. This includes a very ‘hands on’ approach to management, including responsibility for inspections, sensitive data management, client reporting, lease management, service charge budget management, arrears collection, and cashflow management as the fundamentals of property management. The role also includes the management of the complex service charge budgets in line with the SCSI guidelines, assisting with the overseeing of the Commercialisation strategy on site at Blanchardstown Centre, sustainability projects, and supporting the client and Directors with other tasks as and when required. The successful candidate will also be responsible for and have ample opportunities to have good working relationships with retailers, including on debt management, trading performance, discussion plans to refresh a retailers space, providing recommendations to the client etc. An excellent relationship with the Centre Management team will be key, working together to ensure the efficient, effective running of the scheme to bring in footfall and sales in a clean and safe environment. Regular site visits, and making efficient use of time during visits will be a staple part of the role. The candidate will also be required to deliver written output ensuring excellent quality, timeliness, accuracy and completeness, and to mentor, develop and line manage staff as required. **Skills, Knowledge And Experience Required** Strong Property Management experience, and an in\-depth understanding of the challenges currently facing the retail market are essential. You will be a good people manager with a keen eye for detail and the determination to ensure high quality output is achieved at every level. Your excellent relationship skills will enable you to deliver client satisfaction and help to develop the skills of those working alongside you. You will also be able to demonstrate a proactive approach to your work with a continuous willingness to change and improve. Additional skills required are as follows: * Experience in the Property Management sector is essential with proven experience of managing shopping centres or retail parks. * Have a high attention to detail. * A practical pragmatic common sense approach to management. * Must have experience of lease maintenance and an understanding of property accounting. * Must understand the principles and practice of client care. * Excellent time management and organisational skills and use a good level of personal initiative. * Strong team player: builds relationships and consults with others, quickly establishes trust and credibility * Be articulate, professional and persuasive with a good telephone manner and good report writing skills. * Excellent interpersonal and influencing skills. * Show the ability to cross sell other services within the Company. * Ability to work with relatively high degree of autonomy with minimal supervision. * Be SCSI / RICS qualified or in the process of completing / intending to complete qualification. * A solid understanding of ESG and the importance it plays in the built environment. * Be IT competent and ideally have experience with our property management software system Yardi. * Have a full valid driving licence and use of own car. **S** **avills Ireland is an equal opportunity employer.** *Savills aims to attract, recruit and retain the best people from the widest possible pool of talent. As such, we are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse working environment for all employees by striving at all times to be an organisation which does not discriminate, values everyone’s talents and abilities and where diversity is positively promoted.*

title: Associate General Counsel, Civil Litigation
description: Meta seeks a highly proactive and experienced litigator to serve as Associate General Counsel, Civil Litigation. In this role, you will lead and manage a broad docket of complex civil disputes, with particular emphasis on privacy law and GDPR subject\-matter in the context of collective redress, including class and representative\-style actions, as well as multi‑party and cross‑border litigation.You will develop and execute litigation strategy across German courts and international forums, advise internal stakeholders on risk and coordinate closely with cross‑functional partners in Legal, Compliance, Policy, and the business. The successful candidate will have particular expertise in the German legal system but does not have to be based in Germany. We are open to hiring for this role in London, Dublin or Berlin. Associate General Counsel, Civil Litigation Responsibilities: * Owning end‑to‑end litigation strategy and execution for complex civil matters in Germany and across jurisdictions * Expertise in privacy and GDPR\-related litigation. Leading on collective actions, including representative and model proceedings, and coordinating global strategy where cases span multiple jurisdictions * Managing outside counsel to ensure efficient, high‑quality work product, align litigation strategy with business objectives, and drive consistent positions in related matters * Advising on pre‑litigation dispute resolution processes, handling interim relief and injunction practice, and structure effective settlement strategies where appropriate * Advising internal stakeholders on litigation risk and strategy and partnering with Product, Operations, Policy and Communications teams in this context * Overseeing matter budgets, reporting, and governance, ensuring timely updates and clear communication to leadership * Monitoring legal developments in German and EU civil procedure and litigation regimes, and translate those developments into actionable guidance for the business Minimum Qualifications: * Meta requires all employed attorneys to have a law degree or equivalent certificate to permit the practice of law and be appropriately licensed and authorized to practice law for the company in their assigned work location. Unless prohibited under local law, an attorney must hold a current practicing certificate (including where required from the relevant authority in the jurisdiction of the assigned work location) or the local law equivalent bar registration or license as may be applicable * 8\+ years of post‑qualification experience * Qualified lawyer in the EU * Language fluency in German and English * Experience with complex civil litigation in Germany, and substantial cross‑jurisdictional experience managing parallel or related proceedings in other EU or international forums * Experience with German civil procedure and evidence rules * Experience navigating EU‑level collective redress frameworks, with a practical, business‑oriented approach in resolving complex disputes quickly and accurately * Experience working with cross‑functional teams in a fast‑paced, technology‑driven environment * Experience with content, integrity and competition law, including how it is related to tech companies * Advocacy skills and experience Preferred Qualifications: * AI literacy and proficiency as it applies to legal work About Meta: Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics. Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

title: Associate Recruiting Specialist, Hardware
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * 2 years of experience in recruiting, human resources, business development, business operations or account management. * 1 year of recruiting experience at an agency or in\-house recruiting team. **Preferred qualifications:** * 2 years of experience in recruiting Silicon or Hardware Engineers. * 1 year of experience closing candidates and negotiating compensation packages leveraging experience engaging for talent. * Experience solving problems and delivering significant impact as a contributor. * Ability to grow customer relationship management, provide consultative skills, and work in a team environment. * Excellent accountability, work ethic, integrity, and organizational skills with attention to detail. * Excellent communication skills, and the ability to take initiative and build productive relationships. **About The Job** Google's known for our innovative technologies, products and services \-\- and for the people behind them. As part of our recruiting team, you're charged with finding the most interesting candidates who bring an entrepreneurial spirit to all they do. You're responsible for guiding candidates through our hiring process and connecting them to the magic of working at Google. You are creative and driven, which allows you to develop lasting relationships with both candidates and hiring managers. You're also comfortable with numbers and drawing insights from analytics to make our hiring process smarter and more efficient. In this role, you will be a key player in identifying and attracting key talent to Google Cloud in Israel. You will directly support the growth and success of our AI and Infrastructure business in Israel, enabling the Silicon Engineering team to continue leading innovation in its respective product areas. As a Recruiter, you will be responsible for proactively engaging and hiring exceptional candidates for a variety of Silicon Engineering roles, including Design, Design Verification, Physical Design, Silicon Validation, Silicon Engineering and Operations, and additional sub\-domains. You will collaborate closely with hiring managers to understand the specific needs of each role and develop hiring strategies to deliver for the business. Great just isn't good enough for our People Operations team (known elsewhere as "Human Resources"). We bring the world's most innovative people to Google and provide the programs that help them thrive. Whether recruiting the next Googler, refining our core programs, developing talent, or simply looking for ways to inject some more fun into the lives of our Googlers, we bring a data\-driven approach that is reinventing the human resources field. **Responsibilities** * Support candidate assessment for potential roles and identify team alignment based on the organization, function, and role profiles. * Evaluate candidate skills and client needs, screening them for interviews and managing the interview process with some guidance. * Partner with key business leaders, recruiting, and hiring stakeholders to build solutions to defined objectives. * Manage headcount for cross\-project client groups to support accurate tracking and influence headcount allocation, ensure accurate mapping of job postings to open headcount, identify trends/insights to influence hiring strategy and priorities, assist clients with prioritization of requisitions, reconciliation of headcount, and optimum distribution with some guidance. * Develop and execute cross\-project recruiting initiatives to meet established goals and objectives, influence hiring managers on their hiring strategy. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: CSA Engineer Graduate - 2026
description: **About Us** Winthrop is a dedicated Data Centre Construction company providing turnkey solutions to our clients, headquartered in Dublin and delivering throughout Europe. Winthrop specialises in providing Turnkey Data Centre Solutions, providing services from design, through to construction across the full range of civil, structural, architectural, mechanical and electrical services as well as commissioning. **Winthrop Graduate Programme:** Building on the success of our comprehensive Graduate Programme introduced in 2022, we offer an opportunity to work on cutting\-edge projects across Ireland and Europe. This enables graduates to gain valuable technical experience during a crucial stage in their careers. Our programme is uniquely designed to develop skilled professionals with a deep understanding of all phases of large\-scale construction projects, providing graduates with exposure to the complete construction process. **About the Role** We are seeking a motivated **Graduate CSA Engineer** to join our team and gain hands\-on experience in the delivery of civil, structural, and architectural works on major projects. This role offers exposure to all stages of construction, from design coordination to site execution, ensuring compliance with quality, safety, and project timelines **Responsibilities** * Assist in planning and coordination of CSA works on site * Review and interpret technical drawings and specifications * Support site inspections to monitor progress and quality of civil, structural, and architectural works * Liaise with design teams, subcontractors, and suppliers to resolve technical queries * Ensure compliance with health \& safety regulations and project standards * Maintain accurate site documentation, including daily reports and quality records * Assist in risk assessments and method statements for CSA activities * Contribute to problem\-solving and technical solutions during construction **Requirements** * **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Structural Engineering, Architectural Engineering, or a related discipline * Strong understanding of CSA principles and construction processes * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings * Excellent communication and organizational skills * Proficient in MS Office; knowledge of AutoCAD, Revit, or similar software is an advantage * Commitment to health \& safety and quality standards **Benefits** * Mentorship from experienced professionals * Exposure to working on large\-scale data centre projects * A collaborative and supportive work culture * Opportunity to build a strong foundation for your engineering career ***Winthrop Technologies Ltd. is an equal opportunities employer***

title: Calling All Sales Professionals – Transition to B2C Recruitment
description: **Your sales skills are your ticket to success in recruitment.** No prior experience needed \- we’ll teach you everything you need to thrive, while giving you **full control of your income** . 1. **Full structured training:** learn the ropes while you earn, step by step 2. **Clear KPIs:** daily and weekly targets give you focus and measurable wins 3. **Uncapped commission:** the harder you work, the more you take home At **Access** , we’re expanding across Ireland and Northern Ireland. Through structured training and daily mentorship, we turn motivated salespeople into **top\-performing Recruitment Consultants** and future leaders. **What You’ll Get** 1. **Structured, hands\-on training:** learn recruitment like a pipeline you can master step by step 2. **Clear KPIs:** measurable activity, placement, and revenue targets that keep you on track 3. **High earning potential:** your income is directly tied to your performance 4. **Daily coaching \& mentorship:** guidance from experienced consultants until you feel confident 5. **Average Year 1 Compensation** (base \+ commission): **€45,000** \- top consultants often **double their earnings in Year 2** **What You’ll Do** You will focus on three main outcomes: engaging clients, recruiting candidates, and filling shifts. Your daily tasks include: * **Build and grow client relationships:** talk to current and past clients and understand their needs. * **Source top candidates:** find talent through referrals, ads, and outreach * **Match candidates to roles:** understand client requirements and place candidates where they’re needed. * **Coordinate shifts and placements:** ensure candidates are booked correctly, and clients are happy with the schedule. * **Collaborate with the team** : work with business development and account teams to grow client engagement and improve outcomes. **Real People, Real Results** With a background in sales, Amanda earned two promotions in just 11 months. She is now an **Associate Recruitment Consultant** , showing that **focus, determination, and performance** can accelerate your career here \- even if you’re starting with no recruitment experience. **Recruitment is like sales, but better.** Your skills translate directly, your earnings are unlimited, and your career growth is fully supported from day one. For more information, contact \+353 1 572 1159 or email nadia.garcia@accesshealthcare.ie.

title: Civil Site Supervisor
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** The **Civil Site Supervisor** will play a key role in managing the daily operations of multiple civil crews working across various sites. This position requires excellent coordination, communication, and organisational skills to ensure all works are carried out safely, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * Responsible for running numerous projects within urban and rural environments. * Liaising and corresponding with Clients and Client Representatives to enable the successful delivery of projects and building of relationships. * Reviewing drawings with a high level of detail. * Amending designs in line with feedback received. * Create an environment of leadership within the project site team. * Liaising with Contracts Manager and Site Agents to manage work and site resource requirements. * Ensuring customers' expectations are fulfilled whilst building confidence and trust. * Interrogating \& continuously improving project processes. * Apply new technology in an innovative way to create new services and streamline delivery processes. * Mentor designers and identify training needs for junior site team. * Monitor progress of site work, ensuring work is completed on time and within budget. * Planning and overseeing the delivery of materials and manage any discrepancies where appropriate. * Ensuring that all work complies with client requirements and safety legislation alongside other legal requirements. * Responsible for overseeing the traffic management as well as health and safety on\-site and ensuring all safety checks are carried out. * Liaise with Local Authorities with the inspection of footway conditions prior and post installation works. * Liaise with the workflow department regarding the programming and scheduling of work. * Provide Clients with a status report and act as first point of contact for members of the public and sub\-contractors. * Professionally handle complaints received from customers regarding carried out work. * Collaborate with clients to enable them to maximise the value of their infrastructure. * Quality control of works. * Manage required administrative tasks efficiently and effectively. * Ad hoc tasks as required. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Full clean driver's license essential. * A qualification in engineering or equivalent is desirable. * Civils, drainage, or utilities (water \& electricity) experience. * Previous experience and the ability to read designs. * Commercially aware of projects costs. * Experience with site activities and multiple teams. * Good knowledge of HSQE requirements. * Good knowledge of ground support systems. * Computer literate. * Confident, motivated and can work independently as well as part of a team. * Ability to work under pressure with strict deadlines. * Time management abilities to meet targets. * Good communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. * Integrity and respect for confidentiality and privacy. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Claims Analyst
description: We are looking for an experienced Claims Analyst to join our growing business. This is a fast\-paced role in a high\-volume environment, supporting a large patient base across dispensing and claims. The ideal candidate will have strong knowledge of Irish pharmacy schemes, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to work accurately under strict deadlines. This role is suited to someone who is highly organised, a strong communicator, and a team player who can work closely with multiple departments while supporting continuous improvement across the business. **Key Responsibilities** * Keep claims verification processes accurate, up to date, and in line with current requirements * Review, analyse, and manage claims in a high\-volume setting * Liaise with the accounts and billing team to support billing\-related queries and processes * Support bills and customer service functions as required * Work closely with the Training Lead to identify gaps, trends, and opportunities for improvement * Communicate effectively with the pharmacy technician team to provide guidance and support * Analyse claim rejections thoroughly and take the appropriate steps to reclaim where possible * Maintain a comprehensive understanding of Irish pharmacy schemes and claims processes * Ensure compliance with relevant legislation, HSE, and PCRS requirements * Prioritise workload effectively and meet strict deadlines consistently **Requirements** * Previous experience in a claims analyst role or similar pharmacy claims position * Strong working knowledge of Irish pharmacy schemes * Excellent understanding of relevant legislation, HSE, and PCRS processes * Experience dealing with claims verification, rejects analysis, and reclaim processes * Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy * Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Team player with a proactive and solution\-focused approach

title: Clinical Project Manager (CPM)
description: **Key Requirements** * Science and/or medical or nursing background. * Minimum 5 years Clinical Research experience, oncology an advantage, with 4 years’ of project management experience in the clinical trial industry, with a proven track record in managing multi\-center studies. * Proven ability to deliver project goals and mentor junior level employees. * In\-depth knowledge of the Irish clinical trials environment. * Experience with drafting and managing Clinical Trial budgets an advantage. * Excellent communication skills with the ability to represent the company at an international level. **Main Function(s)** * Maintain knowledge and ensure conduct of assigned studies is in compliance with ICH\-GCP, regulatory requirements, applicable guidelines, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), study protocols and project\-specific procedures. * Manage successful execution of multiple projects and lead cross\-functional project teams. **Primary Responsibilities** * Manage assigned studies from concept to final study report in line with ICH\-GCP, regulatory requirements, applicable guidelines, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), study protocols and project\-specific procedures. * Develop and maintain strong relationships with external stakeholders to identify new studies and manage ongoing collaborations. * Contribute to the oversight and execution of key operational strategies and processes. * Lead protocol development for assigned studies and liaise with the CTI Study Start\-up team as required. * Develop and execute study plans including setting project goals, leading risk assessment and management, planning resources and timelines, and study team oversight. * Manage role and project\-specific training. * Overall responsibility for management of essential documents and Trial Master File related to assigned studies. * Oversee the process of site visits and documentation of the visits. * Pre\-emptively identify potential issues and proactively manage resolutions and put in place, where possible, measures to minimise issues and risk for assigned studies. * Contribute to preparation for audits and inspections, conduct, and follow\-up as required both internally and at investigational sites for assigned studies. * Assist as required with drafting and finalising budgets, roles and responsibilities prior to commencing study activities and manage throughout the lifecycle of the study. * Take all reasonable precautions to ensure patient confidentiality is maintained. * Contribute to departmental and cross\-functional SOP development and updates as required. * Update CTPM on the status of all clinical trial activities. * Identify and communicate scientific misconduct according to Cancer Trials Ireland policies. * Perform other tasks as specified by CTPM.

title: Clinical Specialist Sonographer (Paediatrics & DDH)
description: **Job Description** **Position Summary:** The Paediatric Clinical Specialist Sonographer will deliver a high\-quality, child\-centred ultrasound service across Medica Diagnostics Ireland sites. Working autonomously within Medica’s clinical governance framework and agreed protocols, the post holder will independently undertake a wide range of paediatric ultrasound examinations and produce accurate, comprehensive provisional ultrasound reports to support timely clinical decision\-making. The role carries specialist responsibility for maintaining excellence in paediatric imaging practice, including referral vetting, oversight of ultrasound equipment performance, and the consistent application of quality assurance measures. The post holder will contribute to protocol development and review, clinical audit, service evaluation, and continuous improvement activity, while acting as a key point of escalation for complex clinical, operational, or equipment\-related issues within the paediatric ultrasound service. As a senior clinical practitioner, the post holder will also play a central role in education, training, and mentorship. This includes supporting the development of trainee and junior sonographers, student radiographers, and wider imaging colleagues through formal teaching, supervision, and day\-to\-day professional guidance. They will help foster a positive learning culture that promotes clinical excellence, professional growth, and safe paediatric practice. The successful candidate will demonstrate flexibility and leadership in supporting service development, cross\-site working, and future expansion of paediatric ultrasound provision, ensuring delivery remains aligned with Medica Diagnostics Ireland’s strategic and operational priorities. **Key Responsibilities** **Clinical Responsibilities** * To be responsible for independently performing a wide range of high\-quality paediatric diagnostic ultrasound examinations in accordance with Medica Diagnostics Ireland procedures, policies, and clinical protocols. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of clinical and technical developments in paediatric sonography, ensuring evidence\-based practice is reflected in scanning protocols and contributing to the development, review, and refinement of local examination techniques and service standards. * Vet ultrasound referrals to ensure clinical appropriateness and justification in line with professional, legislative, and local guidance. Clarify referral details where required and prioritise patients appropriately according to clinical urgency to support safe and timely care. * Apply advanced clinical judgement and specialist paediatric ultrasound expertise in image acquisition, interpretation, and reporting. Produce high\-quality preliminary ultrasound reports, as locally agreed, and escalate urgent, unexpected, or significant findings without delay to support safe patient management. * Maintain accurate, timely, and complete patient records using Medica’s RIS/PACS and associated clinical systems. Ensure all examinations are correctly annotated, documented, and archived in accordance with organisational standards, professional requirements, and data protection legislation. * Adhere to all Medica Diagnostics Ireland policies and procedures relevant to clinical practice, including safeguarding, infection prevention and control, consent, chaperoning, health and safety, clinical record\-keeping, and communication protocols. * Practise within the limits of personal competence and scope of practice. Seek advice, second opinions, or escalate appropriately to the Head of Ultrasound Services, senior clinical colleagues, or reporting radiologists where clinical complexity, uncertainty, or risk requires. * Carry out daily equipment quality checks and basic troubleshooting of ultrasound systems, ensuring equipment remains safe and fit for purpose. Promptly report faults or malfunctions to the Head of Ultrasound Services and vendor support services and communicate any potential service impact to relevant operational leads. * Ensure a child\-centred, compassionate, and professional approach, adapting communication and examination techniques to meet the needs of infants, children, young people, and their families or carers. * Act as a clinical specialist resource within paediatric ultrasound, supporting safe decision\-making, consistent practice, and high standards of care across the service. **Quality, Education and Training** * Contribute to Medica Diagnostics Ireland’s clinical audit and quality assurance activities, including peer review, image quality checks, and documentation review, supporting high standards in paediatric ultrasound practice. * Support the development and review of paediatric ultrasound protocols, quality control processes, and documentation standards to promote consistency, safety, and best practice across the service. * Participate in clinical governance activities, including audit feedback, incident review, and service improvement initiatives, helping to promote a culture of learning, accountability, and continuous improvement. * Report incidents, near misses, and patient safety concerns promptly through the appropriate reporting systems, supporting effective risk management and safe service delivery. * Provide day\-to\-day guidance and support to trainee and junior sonographers and other relevant staff, contributing to their development and helping to foster a positive learning environment. * Contribute to shared learning through case discussion, reflective practice, and participation in internal education and service development activities. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge and skills in paediatric ultrasound through continuing professional development, mandatory training, and participation in relevant internal and external learning opportunities. * Promote clinical excellence, professional development, and high\-quality child\-centred care across the paediatric ultrasound service. **Professional Conduct And Interpersonal Responsibilities** * Maintain effective, respectful communication with colleagues, radiologists, administrative teams, and non\-radiographic staff across all clinical sites to ensure seamless service delivery. * Adhere to Medica’s Communication Policy and guidelines for operational updates, issue reporting, and MDT coordination. Ensure timely and accurate information flow between sonographers, radiologists, referrers, and clinic staff. * Escalate any issues related to the roster or scheduling to Media Operations manager. * Report complaints or service concerns through the appropriate quality management system, ensuring the Head of Ultrasound is informed in a timely manner. * Represent and promote the Medica brand within clinics through professional behaviour, a collaborative attitude, and a commitment to excellence. * Uphold the highest standards of professionalism by treating every patient with courtesy, empathy, efficiency, and confidentiality, delivering a safe, respectful, and patient\-centred service at all times. **Job Requirements** **Qualifications:** * BSc Diagnostics Radiography * MSc in Medical Ultrasound or PgD in Medical Ultrasound * CORU registered **Essential Skills** * 3 years’ experience in acute paediatric radiology setting * Communication * Patient care * Technical Competency * Attention to detail * Teamwork * Time management **Desirable Skills** * Problem solving * Adaptability * Mentorship and leadership * Audit and Research

title: Commissioning Engineer
description: **Commissioning Engineer – Dublin (Nationwide Travel Required)** Design Build Search is currently seeking a **Commissioning Engineer** based in the greater **Dublin** area to join our clients growing team. The company delivers projects nationwide, and the successful candidate will be comfortable with regular travel across Ireland as required. title: Customer Remediation Summer Intern
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology.
