title: Senior Hydrogeologist
description: **Purpose \& Scope of the Role** Join a well\-established and growing groundwater team within a wider geo\-environmental group of around 100 professionals operating across the UK and Ireland. This role supports the delivery of high\-profile groundwater frameworks and resource development projects, including work with water utilities and public sector clients. As a Consultant or Senior Hydrogeologist, you will focus on technical delivery, project management and the continued growth of groundwater services, contributing to both regional and national projects. **Your Impact in this Position** You will play a key role in developing sustainable groundwater resources, advising on feasibility, investigation, testing and implementation of new water supplies. Your expertise will directly influence abstraction licensing, environmental protection and water resource management. Alongside technical delivery, you will strengthen client relationships, support project delivery and contribute to the ongoing success and expansion of the groundwater business. ### **Core Capabilities** **Technical Delivery** * Deliver hydrogeological assessments from desk study through to reporting and implementation * Design and interpret pumping tests and groundwater investigations * Prepare abstraction licence applications and technical reports * Apply best practice and UK/NI regulatory guidance to groundwater projects **Project \& Commercial Management** * Manage project budgets, programmes and subcontractors * Support tendering, fee proposals and delivery of projects (£100k\+) * Maintain high standards of health, safety and quality across all work **Business Development \& Client Engagement** * Act as a key client contact, building strong working relationships * Support growth through frameworks and new opportunities * Contribute to client meetings, proposals and project delivery strategies **Fieldwork \& Site Leadership** * Plan and supervise field investigations, drilling and testing * Oversee water quality sampling and environmental assessments * Carry out site visits to support technical delivery and problem\-solving **Collaboration \& Mentoring** * Work within a multi\-disciplinary team across geo\-environmental services * Support and mentor junior team members * Contribute to technical excellence and knowledge sharing **Skills, Qualifications \& Experience** A degree in earth sciences, geology or geochemistry, with postgraduate qualification in hydrogeology. Strong experience in groundwater investigations, pumping test analysis and reporting is essential. You will have excellent communication skills, technical judgement and the ability to manage projects and client relationships. Chartered status and experience with modelling tools (e.g. MODFLOW) are advantageous. **Working Environment** This role can be based in Wexford or Belfast, with flexibility to work from other offices for experienced candidates. Projects span Great Britain, Ireland and occasionally international locations, with short periods of travel required. Hybrid and flexible working arrangements are supported, typically combining office, site and remote working. Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. We support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are *Leading with Science®* to solve our clients’ most complex problems. *In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role*

title: Senior Service & Support Engineer
description: **Overview:** The Senior Service \& Support Engineer plays a key role in delivering high\-quality technical support for AIR’s automation solutions. Sitting between the Support Engineer and Support Lead Engineer roles, this position combines strong technical capability with enhanced ownership of service processes, mentoring responsibilities, and cross\-functional collaboration. The ideal candidate will demonstrate deep technical expertise, strong problem\-solving skills, and the ability to drive improvements across support operations while working closely with internal SMEs and engineering teams. **Key Responsibilities:** **Service Operations \& Process Ownership** * Support the refinement of service and support processes, identifying operational gaps and recommending improvements. * Assist in coordinating support resources and activities to meet service demand. * Contribute to the development and implementation of Support Service Agreements (SSA). * Proactively identify opportunities for post\-sales business growth (spare parts, upgrades, reworks, training). **Advanced Technical Support** * Provide senior\-level troubleshooting across software, electrical, and mechanical systems. * Act as an escalation point for Support Engineers, assisting with complex or recurring issues. * Perform detailed root\-cause analysis and propose long\-term corrective actions. * Utilise advanced diagnostic tools and methods to resolve issues efficiently. **Customer \& Internal Stakeholder Interaction** * Engage directly with customers to resolve technical issues and provide clear communication of findings. * Translate complex concepts into accessible explanations for non\-technical stakeholders. * Contribute to high levels of customer satisfaction by ensuring timely and effective resolution of issues. **Collaboration \& Mentoring** * Work closely with internal SMEs (software, electrical, mechanical) to address escalated issues. * Share knowledge, best practices, and lessons learned within the support team. * Mentor junior engineers and technicians to build support capability. **Documentation \& Improvement Initiatives** * Develop and maintain technical documentation including troubleshooting guides, SOPs, and internal knowledge base entries. * Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives across service tools, processes, and customer workflows. * Keep current with industry trends and emerging technologies. **Qualifications \& Experience:** · L6 Craft qualification in Electrical/Mechanical or bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or related field. · Significant experience in technical support roles with exposure to automation systems. · Strong cross\-disciplinary understanding of software, electrical, and mechanical systems. · Experience supporting team members or leading small workstreams is an advantage. **Key Skills \& Attributes** * Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. * Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively across functions. * Customer\-focused mindset with high standards of service delivery. * Proactive, organised, and improvement oriented. **What we offer:** In AIR, we offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees' well\-being and professional development, including company bonus, pension plan, training support, and various wellness initiatives. **About AIR:** AIR is a modern, global manufacturing automation company, newly formed but built on decades of experience from its established business units. We combine deep industry expertise with a dynamic, forward\-looking approach to deliver innovative, reliable automation solutions. We operate across five key sites located in the USA, UK, and Ireland, serving customers worldwide with a unified commitment to quality, speed, and technical excellence. Today, AIR operates as a fully integrated global organization, partnering with customers to help bring their products to life and scale their operations. Our mission is to empower innovation, scalability, and long\-term support by combining the engineering excellence of our people with the operational capabilities of our global facilities. This job description is intended as a summary of the primary responsibilities and qualifications for this position. It is not exhaustive and may be subject to change. AIR is an Equal Opportunities Employer. If you are living in Ireland and hold a valid work permit, we would love to hear from you, if however, you do not hold a valid work permit unfortunately we will not be able to assist you with your job search. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€55,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Senior Structural Engineer
description: We are looking for a professional and reliable Senior Structural Engineer to join our MEA team to lead design packages, provide technical guidance to junior engineers, and ensure design compliance with standards and client expectations. The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring designs meet applicable standards, client requirements, programme, and quality expectations, while contributing to the effective delivery of projects within a multidisciplinary environment. **About Us:** McElroy Associates is a multidisciplinary organisation operating primarily in the pharmaceutical sector providing work in the design, project management and construction supervision of building projects for international and Irish clients. Our experience covers a wide range of projects in new and existing facilities and we offer a comprehensive range of services in Civil, Structural, Mechanical and Electrical Engineering, Architecture, Project Management and Quantity Surveying. McElroy Associates is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration. We are dedicated to providing a workplace free from discrimination and harassment, where everyone is treated with respect and has the opportunity to succeed. **Role Details:** **Location:** Dublin 02, travel to client’s premises required **Salary Range:** €55,000 \- €65,000, considering experience **Hours:** Monday to Thursday 08:30 – 17:15 pm, Friday 16:00 pm Finish **Responsibilities:** * Lead the development of civil and structural designs on small to medium\-scale projects * Produce full design calculations and drawings using hand calculations and appropriate computer software Manage small to medium\-sized projects or defined project packages from design through delivery * Contribute to project planning, programme management, and coordination of deliverables * Liaise with architects, engineers, CAD/Revit technicians, and other consultants during design development * Prepare and contribute to tender documentation and technical reports * Provide technical leadership and guidance to junior and graduate engineers * Prioritise workload effectively and deliver projects to agreed deadlines **Qualifications:** * Degree in Civil Engineering or Structural Engineering * Minimum 5–8 years’ relevant professional experience in an engineering consultancy or similar environment * Chartered Engineer (Engineers Ireland, ICE, or IStructE), or actively working towards chartership * Understanding of project delivery, budget, and programme. * Full working knowledge of British Standards and Eurocodes for Concrete, Steel, Masonry, and Timber * Strong understanding of Irish Building Regulations and construction practices * Proficiency using computer software and be capable of preparing detailed technical designs \& drawings to an exceptional standard * Proficiency in structural analysis and design software, such as: \-TEKLA \-Tedds \-Robot \-STAAD Pro \-QSE or similar **Skills:** * Strong technical competence and proven design leadership ability * Ability to prepare detailed technical designs and drawings to an exceptional standard * Excellent written and verbal communication skills (fluent in English) * Strong organisational and time\-management skills * Ability to prioritise workload and work to tight deadlines * Self\-motivated with strong attention to detail * Confident working with clients and multidisciplinary design teams Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €55,000\.00\-€65,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Private medical insurance * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Structural BIM Technologist

The ideal candidate will be responsible for the preparation of detailed drawings, models, and technical documentation for a variety of civil and structural projects. This role plays a key part in translating engineering concepts into accurate and buildable designs, ensuring compliance with industry standards and project requirements. **About Us:** McElroy Associates is a multidisciplinary organisation operating primarily in the pharmaceutical sector providing work in the design, project management and construction supervision of building projects for international and Irish clients. We are dedicated to providing a workplace free from discrimination and harassment, where everyone is treated with respect and has the opportunity to succeed. **Role Details:** **Location:** Dublin 02, travel to client’s premises may be required **Salary Range:** Depending on Experience, but starting between €38,000 \- €48,000 **Hours:** Monday to Thursday 08:30 – 17:15 pm, Friday 08:30 am – 16:00 pm **Responsibilities:** * Prepare detailed technical drawings and models using CAD software (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit, Civil 3D) * Assist engineers with the design of civil and structural elements * Produce drawings for planning, tender, and construction stages * Ensure all drawings comply with relevant standards and regulations * Coordinate with engineers, architects, and other stakeholders * Maintain accurate project documentation and drawing records * Support site teams with technical queries when required **Qualifications:** * Diploma in Civil/Structural Engineering * Typically 2–5 years’ preparing civil and/or structural drawings * Proficiency in AutoCAD and Revit, with the ability to produce detailed technical drawings to a high standard * Knowledge of relevant design standards and codes * Familiarity with BIM processes **Skills** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise workload and meet tight deadlines * Self\-motivated with excellent attention to detail * Problem\-solving mindset with a proactive approach * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team * Highly detail‑oriented with a strong commitment to producing high‑quality work. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €38,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Private medical insurance * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Talent Acquisition Co-ordinator
description: Vacancy Number: 28204 Category: Human Resources Job Type: Full\-time **Position Description** We are looking to recruit a Talent Acquisition Coordinator to meet the growing demands of the business within the Early Careers Talent Acquisition team. The successful applicant will work in the Talent Acquisition Centre of Excellence “CoE” and will be provided with the support and development needed to enable them to deliver successfully in the role. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage and monitor the advertising of resources in line with the Strategic Resource Plan and approval systems, working closely with HR Governance Specialists. * Coordinate and manage all candidate and applicant communications, interview scheduling, and support line managers and candidates throughout internal and external recruitment processes. * Maintain and update internal trackers and collate data to produce regular management, TA, and HR reports, including monthly recruitment metrics and KPIs. * Assist with Early Talent recruitment, including attending careers fairs, supporting graduate event coordination, and ensuring excellent relationships with all stakeholders. * Review and monitor employment contracts and documentation for external candidates and oversee the new hire process alongside the Onboarding Specialist to ensure compliance with legal and policy requirements. * Maintain the TA SharePoint site, respond to GDPR queries, action data clearance requests, and ensure ongoing compliance with data protection regulations. * Champion continuous improvement initiatives with TA Team Leaders, stay updated on recruitment market trends and employment legislation, and demonstrate flexibility and adaptability to change. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * At least 1\-2 years of relevant work experience. * Ability to meet tight deadlines with accuracy and attention to detail. * Knowledge of HR practices, policies, procedures, and employment legislation. * Proficiency in MS Office, especially advanced Excel, and familiarity with SharePoint. * Strong communication, interpersonal, planning, and organisational skills. * Flexible approach to working. * Ability to work effectively within a team. **Desirable** * A HR qualification or equivalent relevant work experience would be an advantage. * SharePoint and SAP knowledge. **Location** This position will be based in 27 Fitzwilliam Street Lower, Dublin 2\. *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** Early Careers Team Lead **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €33,500\- €40,000 per annum depending on experience **Closing Date** 2nd April 2026 *Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role.* **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Trainee Optical Assistant
description: Are you a caring individual looking to start your career as an Optical Assistant? You’ve come to the right place. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in Swords, our store is a platinum employer and Great Place to Work accredited! **What’s on offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * Full time – 40 hours – weekend working and late nights is essential * Salary – €34,000 OTE * Great career progression opportunities in a friendly store **What we’re looking for?** Some of the responsibilities of this role include: * Welcoming customers into store * Booking in eye tests * Pre\-screening patients * Providing style advice * Measuring frames * Taking phone call **Find out more** If you are comfortable with the responsibilities we’re looking for and are excited by this opportunity, we’re excited to hear from you. Got what we’re looking for? This job will close when it’s filled, so don’t delay, apply today!

title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (SURGERY)
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (SURGERY)** **Department: UCD Beacon Hospital Academy \& UCD School of Medicine** **Reports to:** **Administrative \& Clinical Tutor Responsibilities:** Dr Ruth Pilkington, Director of UCD Beacon Academy, Beacon Hospital **Date: July 2026 (Changeover)** **Available as Full\-time (Tutor \+ Clinical) or Part\-time (Tutor only)** **Overall Purpose of Job** Beacon Hospital has an academic partnership with University College Dublin and one of our principle objectives within this partnership is to support the clinical education programme in the UCD School of Medicine. The clinical tutor role plays an instrumental role in identifying key teaching moments for our undergraduate students who are attached to specialities as part of their experiential learning. All clinical tutors are part of a vibrant community of academic staff who pass on their clinical experience and expertise to the next generation of medical professionals. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables: Clinical Tutor (Surgery)** 1\.1\. Coordinate and participate in the teaching and training of the students in Beacon Hospital. 1\.1\.1\. Provide tutorial support and facilitate shared learning experiences for students while on placements. 1\.1\.2\. Provide lectures as part of the formal clinical curriculum and assist with the preparation of clinical examinations. 1\.1\.3\. Provide a level of supervision of clinical activity appropriate to the competence and experience of the individual student. 1\.1\.4\. Utilise a range of appropriate methods of teaching and assessment to demonstrate clinical learning opportunities \- i.e. OSCE style tutorials, problem\-based learning and bed\-side tutorials. 1\.2\. Liaise with relevant UCD personnel to ensure that clinical placements satisfy academic requirements and ensure integrated delivery of all courses. 1\.3\. Coordinate and partake in the orientation and appraisal of medical students attending Beacon Hospital. 1\.4\. Partake in the competency assessment of interns using the national intern competency framework tools within 30 working days of the intern's commencement date. 1\.5\. Demonstrate leadership by assisting in the professional and personal development of the students and Interns throughout their clinical placement at the Beacon Hospital through promotion of self\-learning, formal lectures and tutorials. 1\.6\. Act as a resource for students and Interns seeking specialty information and guidance. 1\.7\. Ensure that the relevant information about progress and performance is made available to the Beacon Hospital and UCD student placement coordinator. 1\.8\. Act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of the UCD Beacon Hospital Academy. 1\.9\. Adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI standards, Code Blue, Infection Prevention and Control, Falls Reduction and data protection. 1\.10\. Contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall clinical education is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. 1\.11\. Build upon the library of tutorial content available in the hospital. The tutor is required to prepare tutorials that can be used in each of the module s \- i.e. Surgery A/B and Clin Res 1, and that can be reproduced with ease each year by clinical tutors at the Beacon Hospital. These may include high level overviews of certain topics that can be taught to students with supporting artefacts, media, and learning tools. 1\.12\. Engage actively in research and scholarly activity as appropriate to the school or hospital. 1\.13\. Champion the student and Interns' participation in clinical audits to ensure that UCD Beacon Hospital Academy programmes meet quality and patient safety standards and any other applicable performance measures. 1\.14\. Report to the appointed clinical lead consultant within the Beacon in all matters relating to medical students and Interns in the manner requested by said clinical lead. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Current relevant Full Irish Medical Council Registration. MRCS/Membership of relevant college is desirable **Experience** Minimum 3 years postgraduate experience in an acute hospital setting. Experience in clinical education of students and junior staff. Experience in the effective management of a poor performing staff member and or student. Attendance at clinical tutor trainee days is required (if not complete the successful candidate will be given the opportunity to attend.) **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** High\-level interpersonal and communication skills (both oral and written) facilitating effective interaction within the work areas, the university and other institutions is a mandatory requirement. Strong organisational skills, ability to prioritise work, to work to deadlines and to use own initiative is a mandatory requirement. Proven experience of working in a quality assurance/improvement environment is a mandatory requirement. Proven ability to work as part of a team is required. Ability to embrace change in a dynamic environment, willingness to adopt new procedures and undertake further appropriate training as required Experience in teaching and examining at third level is desirable. Experience in curriculum development and the development of clinical education programmes is desirable. Evidence of research and peer reviewed publications is desirable. Good knowledge of university rules, regulations and procedures, especially those pertaining to examinations, registration and student welfare is required. Experience in research development at third level is desirable. Experience of general and academic administration is desirable. Display awareness and appreciation of the service users and the ability to empathise with and treat others with dignity and respect. Demonstrate sufficient clinical knowledge and evidence\-based practice to carry the out duties and responsibilities of the role. Demonstrate an ability to apply knowledge to best practice standards. All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: UKI Senior Paid Search Executive
description: ### **UKI****Senior Paid Search Executive** We’re looking for a Senior Paid Search Executive to join our Search team at Flutter UK \& Ireland. This role plays a critical part in driving high quality acquisition at scale across the UK \& Ireland through best\-in\-class paid search execution. You’ll take senior ownership of performance across Google Ads, Microsoft/Bing, and Apple Search Ads, helping to shape strategy, lead optimisation, and deliver sustained commercial growth across some of Flutter’s most recognisable brands. Success in this role will be measured through your ability to scale profitable volume, improve CPA and LTV, and continuously evolve our paid search approach through testing, automation and innovation. As a Senior Paid Search Executive, you’ll be a core member of the central UK \& Ireland Search team, working across a broad portfolio of market\-leading Flutter brands including Betfair, Paddy Power and Sky Betting \& Gaming. You’ll combine hands\-on campaign ownership with a more strategic lens, supporting not just execution but also the evolution of our approach to paid search at scale. You’ll play a key role in shaping best practice across the channel, mentoring junior team members, and working closely with Paid Search Managers and cross\-functional stakeholders to ensure our activity is delivering against clear commercial and customer outcomes. The role sits at the intersection of performance, automation, creative testing and commercial strategy, with accountability across always\-on acquisition, trading moments, and new product or market launches. This is a high impact role offering the opportunity to influence major budgets in a fast\-paced, performance\-driven environment. ### **What you’ll do** * Lead the day\-to\-day management, optimisation and scaling of Paid Search activity across Google Ads, Microsoft/Bing and Apple Search Ads * Take ownership of performance against CPA, volume, LTV and wider commercial targets * Shape bid strategies, budget allocation, account structure and automation frameworks * Drive continuous testing across keywords, creative formats and platform innovation * Provide hands\-on leadership and guidance to more junior team members within the Search team * Work closely with Senior Search Managers to plan channel strategy aligned to UK \& Ireland business priorities * Partner with brand, product, analytics and commercial teams to unlock performance opportunities * Deliver clear insight, performance narratives and recommendations to senior stakeholders * Support major trading moments, regulatory changes and strategic growth initiatives across the region ### **How you’ll do it** Strong proven experience managing Paid Search at scale across Google Ads and Microsoft/Bing (Apple Search Ads experience is highly desirable) * Deep understanding of performance marketing principles including CPA, ROAS, LTV, incrementality and attribution * Experience managing seven\-figure budgets in fast\-paced, high\-volume environments * Advanced optimisation, testing and automation capability * Highly data\-driven with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Confident acting as a senior stakeholder within a cross\-functional environment * Strong communication skills with the ability to influence at multiple levels * Experience within gaming, gambling, fintech, subscription or other regulated digital industries is desirable ### **What’s in it for you:** * Flexible ways of working – home or office, it’s your choice! * £1,000 learning fund * Twice\-yearly bonus (with part of it guaranteed!) * Pension contribution scheme * Private healthcare * Access to thousands of Udemy courses * Invest via the Company Sharesave Scheme * 16 hours paid volunteering time per year * Unlimited holiday ### **About Flutter** Flutter is the world’s leading online sports betting and iGaming operator, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. Our ambition is to change our industry for the better, making use of our significant scale and challenger mentality. By Changing the Game, we believe we can deliver long\-term growth while promoting a positive, sustainable future for the industry. We are well\-placed to do so through the distinctive, global advantages of the Flutter Edge, which gives our brands access to group\-wide benefits to stay ahead of the competition, as well as our clear vision for sustainability through our Positive Impact Plan. Flutter operates a diverse portfolio of leading online sports betting and iGaming brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, tombola, Betfair, MaxBet, Junglee Games and Adjarabet. ### **About Flutter UK \& Ireland** The UK \& Ireland region of Flutter unites some of the biggest brands in the betting and gaming industry; Betfair, Paddy Power, PokerStars, Sky Betting \& Gaming and tombola. At Flutter UK \& Ireland, we strive for the next level and drive innovation to set the pace as leaders, putting our **customers first, always** . We **win together** through team spirit and unparalleled dedication. When we’re **free to be ourselves** , we thrive and unleash our unique talents —creating a culture that empowers our people to **change the game.** We see opportunity everywhere and there is always more to discover... **We’re working to be an inclusive employer. We encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.** **If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.**

title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Practitioner \- Small Animal** ------------------------------------------- **Anicare Vets, Blanchardstown, Dublin** ---------------------------------------- **Full\-time, Permanent (Part\-time considered)** ------------------------------------------------- **Salary up to €75,000 DOE** ---------------------------- ### **A Role Where You Can Truly Thrive** This is an opportunity to join a practice where your clinical skills are valued, your development is supported, and your work\-life balance is genuinely respected. At Anicare Blanchardstown, we’re looking for a small animal veterinary surgeon who wants more than just a job \- someone who enjoys being part of a collaborative team, takes pride in delivering high standards of care, and is keen to continue growing professionally in a well\-equipped, modern environment. Whether you’re looking to deepen your clinical interests, develop a niche, or simply work somewhere you feel supported and trusted, this is a role where you can settle in and thrive. ### **A Balanced and Thoughtful Working Pattern** This role is structured around an average 40\-hour working week, designed to provide both strong clinical exposure and protected time to recharge. In practice, your weekly rota will typically be built across 4 days, with some weeks closer to 36 hours and others slightly higher, depending on the weekend rota. This balances out across the rota cycle to an average of 40 hours per week. The weekend rota is fair and clearly structured, averaging approximately 1\.5 weekends in every 6 weeks, and is factored into your overall hours. There are no out\-of\-hours or on\-call requirements, and bank holiday commitments are minimal, usually limited to one or two per year. The overall aim is to provide a sustainable, predictable working pattern that supports both clinical quality and a healthy work\-life balance. ### **A Clinic Designed for Quality Care** Our Blanchardstown practice has recently undergone refurbishment and is equipped to a high standard, allowing you to work cases thoroughly and confidently. You’ll have access to dedicated surgical and dental theatres, digital and dental x\-ray, ultrasound, and a fully equipped in\-house laboratory. We’re particularly proud of our feline\-friendly approach, with separate dog and cat wards and a dedicated cat consult room \- making this an especially appealing environment for anyone with an interest in feline medicine. The caseload is varied, with a healthy mix of medical and surgical work, and excellent scope for someone with a passion for dentistry to really make the most of the facilities available. ### **A Team That Works Together** You’ll be joining a close\-knit and experienced team of four vets, four nurses, and two support staff. Collaboration is a big part of how we work. There is always someone to discuss cases with, share ideas, or simply support each other through a busy day. Within the team, there is strong clinical expertise, including advanced knowledge in cardiology and diagnostic imaging, giving you access to valuable support and learning opportunities. As part of the wider Anicare Veterinary Group and IVC network, you’ll also benefit from broader clinical communities, mentorship, and long\-term career pathways. ### **Who Will Fit In Here** This role would suit a vet with at least a couple of years’ experience in small animal practice who is confident with routine surgery and consultations. It’s not a position suited to a new graduate, as there will be elements of autonomy required. You must be VCI registered and eligible to work in Ireland. We’re looking for someone approachable, team\-oriented, and motivated to continue developing their skills. An interest in feline medicine or dentistry would be a real advantage, but most importantly, you should enjoy good\-quality general practice and working as part of a supportive team. ### **A Package That Supports You Long\-Term – What We Offer** Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll be supported with a comprehensive benefits package designed around your wellbeing and career progression: #### **Work Life Balance \& Employee Wellbeing** * 22 days annual leave * Additional holiday entitlement for your birthday * Paid maternity, paternity, and adoptive leave packages * Cycle to Work Scheme * Company Sick Pay \& Bereavement Leave * Access to EAP \& Wellbeing Centre #### **Career Development** * €1300 annual CPD allowance with 40 hours paid CPD leave * Full funding for any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Opportunities for clinical and leadership progression * VCI and VDS memberships fully covered * Dedicated CARE fund \- €1,100 annually to support exceptional cases #### **Additional Benefits** * Annual Pay Reviews * Employer Pension Contributions * Discounted Veterinary Fees across the IVC Evidensia network * Free Flu Vaccinations * Access to “Pawesome” Benefits – retail and lifestyle discount platform * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) ### **Let’s Have a Conversation** If you’re looking for a role where you can do your best clinical work, feel part of a genuinely supportive team, and enjoy a balanced lifestyle in Dublin, we’d love to hear from you. Get in touch to find out more \- even if you’re just curious, we’re always happy to have an informal chat. Up to €75,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Vice President, Internal Audit - EMEA
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Vice President, Internal Audit \- EMEA**What does a successful VP of Audit do at Fiserv?** At Fiserv, a successful Internal Audit Vice President will guide and oversee various audit engagements and initiatives. You will leverage your extensive experience in auditing and leadership to provide strategic guidance, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and business objectives. **What you will do:** * Execute a risk\-based audit strategy aligned with Fiserv's business goals. * Lead and manage the Internal Audit team, providing strategic oversight and guidance. * Oversee complex audit engagements ensuring compliance with applicable standards. * Provide leadership and guidance to the audit planning process. * Foster strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including regulators. * Ensure compliance with governance, risk management, and control standards. * Drive continuous improvement in audit processes and methodologies. * Acts as an escalation point for complex issues and implement measures to remediate. * Mentor and develop audit staff to promote professional growth. **What you will need to have:** * 12\+ years of experience in audit with 5\+ years leading audit teams. * Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, information systems, or business administration and/or equivalent military experience. * Demonstratable knowledge of Global Internal Auditing Standards and latest audit techniques. * Strong communication skills with a proven ability to build rapport and interact with cross\-functional teams. * Certified Internal Auditor (CIA), Certified Public Accountant (CPA), or equivalent certifications. **What would be great to have:** * Advanced degree in a related field. * Extensive experience in financial services or fintech industry. * Knowledge of global laws, regulations, and standards, particularly in highly regulated environments. * Experience leveraging data analytics within the audit function. Thank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please: * Apply using your legal name * Complete the step\-by\-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable). **Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:** Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law. **Note to agencies:** Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions. **Warning about fake job posts:** Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.

title: Business Analyst
description: **About Waystone** Waystone is a leading asset\-servicing solutions provider of institutional governance, administration, risk and compliance services to financial institutions. With over 25 years’ experience and a comprehensive range of specialist services to its name, Waystone helps our clients structure, operate and grow through our expertise, innovation and digitisation, backed by the operational scale to support global expansion. **Summary:** Reporting to the Business Systems Analyst Lead, the Business Analyst will be an integral part of the team’s success. The roles is part of a dedicated team of progressive professionals with extensive industry experience, focused on cultivating a positive culture, delivering high\-quality work and championing digital and data transformation. The Business Analyst will play a hands‑on role working with internal stakeholders and the Enterprise Change Team. The role focuses on eliciting, analysing and translating business requirements into high‑quality user stories and functional specifications that support BAU, enhancements and strategic initiatives across the Salesforce platform. This dynamic individual applies their valuable experience in a fast\-paced global organisation and is a key contributor to the digital and data landscape of this company, strengthening our business and clients’ experience. **ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES** * Utilise skills and experience in the areas of structured problem solving, business analysis, data analytics, design thinking, and lean process improvement to support performance excellence in the organisation. * Elicit business requirements from stakeholders, based on project goals and objectives, supported by factual cost benefit analysis producing intelligent and strategic Business Cases for Executive level consideration. * Collaborate inside project structures, as well as work independently, and engage directly with senior stakeholders to drive outcomes, including the facilitation of business interviews and workshops using proven techniques. * Analyse end\-to\-end operational processes to identify automation and AI opportunities using structured prioritisation criteria. * Redesign processes to be automation\-ready, clearly separating deterministic steps, AI\-enabled interpretation, and human judgement points. * Assist and supervise team contributions and communications to ensure complete and holistic requirement coverage. Help ensure that requirements, considerations and process are fit for purpose, creating detailed business, functional, non\-functional and operating model requirements, supporting the design, build, and test of changes and business readiness/training activities. * Map full business processes, provide solution options \& benefit analysis and implement solutions for digital and lean process optimization improvements. * Manage and coordinate multiple phases of technical areas within a project and/or multiple small projects of moderate scope and sometimes complex assignments from business case development to project execution \& transition to BAU. * Support Rapid Deployment Pods (RDPs) through discovery, testing, and implementation * Apply proven design thinking standards and lean methodologies. Provide expertise and support on use of methods and tools. * Act as a digital and AI change champion within Operations. * Lead task planning, process/data analysis and modelling and/or logical database design with relational databases. * Identify and assess risks to the success of a project and proactively communicate and mitigate them. * Track activities against the project schedule and manage stakeholder involvement as appropriate. * Be a digital and data change champion and handle training and digital adoption and build competency within the practice. **REQUIREMENTS** To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Experience: * Significant Business Analyst experience, ideally within the funds industry (preferred) or broader financial services industry, in highly regulated environments. * Proven track record in the delivery of digital, automation, or operational transformation initiatives. * Expertise in business analysis methodologies and awareness of new and emerging technologies. * Excellent process mapping skills and worked on projects that have required process improvements, experienced in MS Visio. * Strong applications/technology knowledge, building on specialist domain knowledge. * Experience in applying problem\-solving methodologies and tools to diagnose and solve operational problems. * Experience working with low\-code / no\-code automation platforms. * Proven ability in assuring project meets quality standards by providing guidance and developing procedures relating to product quality assurance, testing planning, creation and execution. * Advanced data analytical skills and outstanding problem\-solving capabilities, particularly with loosely defined structure and scope. * Experience with delivering change projects for CRMs such as Salesforce. * Familiarity with M365 Copilot and AI\-assisted productivity tools * Awareness of Power Automate, n8n, or similar orchestration platforms * Deep knowledge of core business operations developed through executing projects in areas like strategy, operating model design, lean process improvement, and both waterfall and agile digital transformation and delivery. * Ability to work collaboratively in a team, and coach and support development of more junior colleagues. * Demonstrable professional integrity and dedication to upholding the company’s high\-quality standards and customer service focus. * Proven adherence to legal and regulatory guidelines, and in\-house policies and business ethics. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to communicate at both senior management and end user level. * Must be highly motivated, self\-driven, proactive and seek continuous learning opportunities. Education: * Third level qualification, or equivalent work experience * Certified in Business Analysis, or equivalent * Certified in Six Sigma , or equivalent * Project management qualification and/or experience desirable
